def onValueChange(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called whenever the text component's value parameter changes for any reason including typing
	in the text window or changing the parameter directly. The callback is equivalent to the
	onValueChange in the parameter exectute DAT.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonValueChange comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return
	
def onFocus(comp):
	"""
	Called when one of the text components viewers gets keyboard focus. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocus comp:', comp.path)
	return

def onFocusEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when one of the text component's viewers loses keyboard focus. In regular editing mode
	this is right after the contents are saved back to the value parameter. This function is called
	everytime the viewer loses focus, even if the text does not change.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		info: a dictionary containing information about the event including:
			reason: a string indicating what caused the viewer to lose focus e.g. 'enter', 'escape', 'tab', 'unknown'
	
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocusEnd comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	return
	
def onTextEdit(comp):
	"""
	Called each time the contents of the text component viewer change i.e. when typing a character,
	deleting text, cutting or pasting. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEdit comp:', comp.path, '- text: ', comp.editText)
	return
	
def onTextEditEnd(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called when the user finishes editing and the value has been changed. Not called in 'Continuous Edit' mode. Use
	onTextEdit and onFocusEnd when using continuous editing.

    Note: Any Python undo blocks created by this callback will be included in the undo step for the change to the text field.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEditEnd comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )

*�����������������%def onFrameEnd(self):
	"""
	Only UI frame update while subprocess is running.
	Subprocess terminating should be handled in watcher threads Success or Exception hooks.
	"""
	runningProcess = ext.TDPyEnvManagerExt.Helper.runningProcess
	if runningProcess or ext.TDPyEnvManagerExt.Working:
		result = runningProcess.poll() if runningProcess else None
		if ext.TDPyEnvManagerExt.Working:
			op('circle1').par.borderalpha = 1
			op('circle1').par.rotate += -1

		elif result is not None:
			ext.TDPyEnvManagerExt.SubprocessTerminated(result)
			

	return

*�����������������٧"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner components with python. An
extension is accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from any operator
within the extended component. If the extension is promoted via its
Promote Extension parameter, all its attributes with capitalized names
can be accessed externally, e.g. op('yourComp').PromotedFunction().

Help: search "Extensions" in wiki
"""
import pathlib
import os
import platform
import traceback
import types
from typing import Type
import yaml
import logging

from packaging.version import Version
import TDFunctions as TDF

class TDPyEnvManagerExt:
	"""
	TDPyEnvManagerExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):	
		"""
		This is the extension for the TDPyEnvManager component.

		The TDPyEnvManager is a custom TouchDesigner component that helps 
		manage Python environments as well as Conda environments and helps
		TouchDesigner users and developers integrate easily and efficiently 
		third party Python libraries that are installed in an external environment.

		Users can rely on this component to create, activate,
		and manage Python vEnvs and Conda environments.
		Users can easily get to the activated environment's CLI and 
		use pip or conda commands to install packages.
		Users can also export the environment to a requirements.txt or environment.yml file, 
		and create a new environment from it.

		Selected environments are added to the TouchDesigner Python path and
		packages installed in the environment are available to TouchDesigner.

		Additionally, the Conda Env mode allows for users to search for environments
		available globally on the system, granted a valid conda installation is found
		and the environments are matching the Python version of the TouchDesigner installation.
		
		NOTE: Sideloading Python environments and third party packages is at your own risk 
		and can cause instabilities. For more details, head to https://docs.derivative.ca/Python

		Args:
			ownerComp (COMP): The current instance of the component on which the extension is assigned.

		Attrs:
			ownerComp (COMP): The current instance of the component on which the extension is assigned.
			Logger (COMP): Logger COMP for logging messages used in the TDPyEnvManager extension.
			SafeLogger (logging.Logger): SafeLogger, Logger COMP's python logger object, for logging messages safely across threads.
			ThreadManager (ThreadManager COMP): A reference to TouchDesigner's ThreadManager for creating TDTasks and running them in threads.
			Helper (TDPyEnvManagerHelper): Helper class for managing Python environments and Conda environments with only Python and no reference to TouchDesigner objects.
			Context (dict): Context dictionary containing information about the environment.
			ContextLock (threading.Lock): Lock for the Context dictionary to ensure thread safety.
			Ready (bool): Flag to indicate if the TDPyEnvManager environment is ready to be used.
			Working (bool): Flag to indicate if the TDPyEnvManager is currently working on a (potentially threaded) task.
			useCondaEnvYml (bool): Flag to indicate if a Conda environment YAML file should be used.
			refreshCondaEnvListPayload (dict): Payload for refreshing the Conda environment list in a thread. This get set from the RefreshCondaEnvironmentsList method.
			disclaimerRead (bool): Flag to indicate if the disclaimer has been read.
			disclaimerResult (bool): Result of the disclaimer acceptance.
			condaTosRead (bool): Flag to indicate if the Conda terms of service have been read.
			condaTosResult (bool): Result of the Conda terms of service acceptance.
		"""
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.Logger = self.ownerComp.op('logger')
		self.SafeLogger = self.Logger.Logger
		self.ThreadManager = op.TDResources.ThreadManager

		projectFolderAsPath = pathlib.Path(project.folder)
		
		self.Helper = app.pyEnvHelper
		helperLogger = self.Helper.logger
		helperLogger.parent = self.Logger.Logger
		helperLogger.propagate = True
		helperLogger.setLevel(logging.NOTSET)

		for handler in list(helperLogger.handlers):
			helperLogger.removeHandler(handler)

		if self.Helper.loadedFromContext:
			self.ownerComp.par.Active = self.Helper.startAsActive
			self.ownerComp.par.Mode = self.Helper.mode
			if self.Helper.mode == 'Conda Env':
				self.ownerComp.par.Condainstallfolder = pathlib.Path(self.Helper.installPath).relative_to(projectFolderAsPath)
				self.ownerComp.par.Environment = self.Helper.envName

		else:
			self.Helper.mode = self.ownerComp.par.Mode.eval()
		
		envName = self.Helper.validateEnvName(self.Helper.appendVEnvSuffix(projectFolderAsPath.name) if self.Helper.mode == 'Python vEnv' else self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval())
		
		if self.Helper.mode == 'Conda Env' and self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval() != envName:
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment = envName

		self.Helper.envName = envName

		parPath = self.ownerComp.par.Condainstallfolder.eval() if platform.system() == 'Windows' else self.ownerComp.par.Condainstallfolder.eval().replace('\\', '/')
		self.Helper.installPath = self.Helper.setPythonRoot(projectFolderAsPath) if self.Helper.mode == 'Python vEnv' else self.Helper.setCondaRoot(pathlib.Path(parPath))
				
		op('circle1').par.borderalpha = 0
		op('circle1').par.rotate = 0	
		self.Working = False
		
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'Ready', value=False, dependable=True,
						   readOnly=False) 
				
		self.useCondaEnvYml = False
		self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload = None

		self.disclaimerRead = False
		self.disclaimerResult = None
		self.condaTosRead = False
		self.condaTosResult = None

		self.Logger.Debug('TDPyEnvManager Init done.')

		self.postInit()

	def postInit(self) -> None:
		"""
		Once the extension is initilized, trigger TouchDesigner specific routines.

		Multiple checks are being done to ensure proper initialization of the extension and 
		validation for the component to be used.

		External variables can override the default values of the component and have an impact
		during initialization.

		TD_CONDA_INSTALL: Path to the conda installation folder. This will set the paramaeter Condainstallfolder as well
		as forcing the component to be in Conda mode.
		TD_CONDA_ENV: Name of the conda environment to be used. This will set the parameter Environment as well 
		as forcing the component to be Active if it was not already.

		If none of those variables are set, the component will check if the Active parameter is set to True.
		If the component is active, it will check if the mode is set to Python vEnv or Conda Env and proceed to add to the search path 
		the Python vEnv when set to Python vEnv mode.
		If the mode is set to Conda Env, it will check if the conda installation path is valid and add it to the search path.
		It will proceed to check if the conda environment is valid and add it to the search path as well.
		"""
		isCompActive = self.ownerComp.par.Active.eval()

		if 'TD_CONDA_INSTALL' in os.environ:
			# An env variable is set for a conda install path to be used.
			# Switch to conda mode, set env var as conda install path.
			self.ownerComp.par.Mode = 'Conda Env'
			self.ownerComp.par.Condainstallfolder = os.environ['TD_CONDA_INSTALL'] if platform.system() == 'Windows' else os.environ['TD_CONDA_INSTALL'].replace('\\', '/')
			self.Logger.Info(f'Conda install path set from env var. Overriding exsiting value to {os.environ["TD_CONDA_INSTALL"]}.')

		if 'TD_CONDA_ENV' in os.environ:
			# An env variable is set for a conda env to be used.
			# Switch to conda mode, set env var as conda env.
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment = os.environ['TD_CONDA_ENV']
			self.Logger.Info(f'Conda env name set from env var. Overriding exsiting value to {os.environ["TD_CONDA_ENV"]}.')

			if not isCompActive:
				# We are bypassing active flag because the env var was set.
				self.ownerComp.par.Active = True
				self.Logger.Info('Component was not active, but env var was set. Setting Active to True and processing with init sequence.')

		if not isCompActive:
			self.Logger.Info('Component is not active. Set Active to True to start.')
			self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Toggle the Active par to start.'
			return

		elif self.ownerComp.par.Mode.eval() == 'Python vEnv':
			# Check if env at path exists
			envPath = self.Helper.getPythonEnvPath(self.Helper.envName)
			if self.Helper.validatePath(envPath):
				# vEnv is valid, link it.
				success = self.Helper.linkEnv(envPath)
				
				if success:
					self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Environment linked and ready.'
				else:
					self.Logger.Error(f'Python vEnv path {envPath} was not valid or could not be resolved. Aborting init. Create vEnv and restart project.')
					self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid vEnv, create vEnv to continue.'
								
			else:
				self.Logger.Error(f'Python vEnv path {envPath} was not valid or could not be resolved. Aborting init. Create vEnv and restart project.')
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid vEnv, create vEnv to continue.'
			
			return	

		elif self.ownerComp.par.Mode.eval() == 'Conda Env':
			condaInstallPath = self.Helper.getCondaRoot()
			isCondaLinked = False
			if self.Helper.validatePath(condaInstallPath):
				isCondaLinked = self.Helper.LinkConda(condaInstallPath)

				if isCondaLinked:
					self.Logger.Debug('Conda available.')
			
			else:
				self.Logger.Error('Could not find a valid conda install. Update install path or create env to continue.')
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid conda install. Update install path or create env to continue.'
			
			if isCondaLinked:
				envPath = self.Helper.getCondaEnvPath(self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval())
				if self.Helper.validatePath(envPath):
					# Conda env is valid, link it.
					success = self.Helper.linkEnv(envPath)
					if success:
						self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Environment linked and ready.'
					else:
						self.Logger.Error(f'Conda env path {envPath} was not valid or could not be resolved. Create Conda env.')
						self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid conda env, create env to continue.'

				else:
					self.Logger.Error(f'Conda env path {envPath} was not valid or could not be resolved. Create Conda env.')
					self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid conda env, create env to continue.'

			# More post init work
			self.RefreshCondaEnvListThreaded()

		return

	def createCondaEnvThreaded(self) -> None:
		"""
		Create a Conda environment in a thread, using the Helper class.
		Use the environment.yml file if it exists and the env creation was triggered from
		the Createfromenvironmentyml pulse, otherwise create a barebone conda environment with the name
		set in the Environment parameter.
		"""
		if self.useCondaEnvYml:
			asCondaEnvDict = None
			envYmlFile = pathlib.Path.cwd() / 'environment.yml'
			if not envYmlFile.exists():
				self.Logger.Error(f'Environment yml file not found at {envYmlFile}. Aborting.')
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Environment yml file not found. Aborting.'
				return
			
			with open(envYmlFile, 'r') as file:
				asCondaEnvDict = yaml.safe_load(file)

			if not asCondaEnvDict:
				self.Logger.Error(f'Environment yml file is empty or invalid. Aborting.')
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Environment yml file is empty or invalid. Aborting.'
				return

			envName = self.Helper.validateEnvName(asCondaEnvDict.get('name', self.Helper.envName))
			self.Helper.envName = envName
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment = self.Helper.envName
			deps = asCondaEnvDict.get('dependencies', [])
			for dep in deps:
				if isinstance(dep, str) and dep.startswith('python='):
					self.Helper.pythonVersion = dep.split('=')[1]
					break
		
		if self.Helper.envName == '' and self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval() == '':
			self.Helper.envName = self.Helper.appendVEnvSuffix(pathlib.Path(project.folder).name)
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment = self.Helper.envName

		myReader = self.ThreadManager.TDTask(
			target=self.Helper.createCondaEnv,
			SuccessHook=self.createCondaEnvSuccess,
			ExceptHook=self.createCondaEnvExcept,
			RefreshHook=self.createCondaEnvRefresh,
			args=(self.Helper.installPath, self.Helper.envName, self.Helper.pythonVersion, self.useCondaEnvYml)
		)
		self.ThreadManager.EnqueueTask(myReader)
		self.Working = True
		
		self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Creating Conda Env...'
		self.Logger.Info(f'Creating Conda Env {self.Helper.envName} at {self.Helper.installPath}...')
		
		return

	def createCondaEnvSuccess(self) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the Conda environment creation is successful.
		Add the Conda environment to the search path.
		"""
		self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Conda Env created successfully. Linking Conda Env...'
		self.Logger.Info('Conda Env created successfully. Linking Conda Env...')

		isCondaLinked = self.Helper.LinkConda(self.Helper.getCondaRoot())

			self.Logger.Error('Conda could not be linked.')

		self.Helper.runningProcess = None

		self.useCondaEnvYml = False

		self.Working = False
		self.resetUICue()

	def getGlobalEnvs(self, localEnvDir: pathlib.Path = None) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
		"""
		Get the list of global conda environments that are not relative to the local environment directory.
		These environments must match the Python version of the TouchDesigner installation.

		Args:
			localEnvDir (pathlib.Path, optional): Path to the env directory of the currently linked conda installation. Defaults to None.

		Returns:
			list[tuple[str, str]]: A list of tuples containing the environment name and path.
		"""
		globalList = []
		condaEnvManager = importlib.import_module('conda.core.envs_manager')
		condaEnvMod = importlib.import_module('conda.env.env')
		globalEnvs = condaEnvManager.list_all_known_prefixes()
		
		for env in globalEnvs:
			env = pathlib.Path(env).resolve()
			
			if not env.is_relative_to(localEnvDir):
				asCondaEnv = condaEnvMod.from_environment(env.name, str(env))
				
				envVersion = None
				
				asCondaEnvDict = asCondaEnv.to_dict()
				deps = asCondaEnvDict.get('dependencies', [])
				for dep in deps:
					if isinstance(dep, str) and dep.startswith('python='):
						envVersion = dep.split('=')[1]
						break
				
				if not envVersion or not self.Helper.pythonVersion:
					continue

				envVersion = Version(envVersion)
				TDVersion = Version(self.Helper.pythonVersion)
				match = envVersion.major == TDVersion.major and envVersion.minor == TDVersion.minor
				if match:
					globalList.append((env.name + "*", str(env)))		
		
		return globalList

	def refreshCondaEnvironmentsList(self, condaInstallPath: pathlib.Path, projectFolder: str, includeGlobal: bool) -> None:
		"""
		Refresh the list of conda environments available in the conda installation path.

		Args:
			condaInstallPath (pathlib.Path): Path to the current conda installation folder.
			projectFolder (str): Path to the current project folder.
			includeGlobal (bool): Flag to include global conda environments in the list.
		"""
		if self.Helper.validatePath(condaInstallPath):
			envsDir = condaInstallPath / 'envs'
			subdirs = envsDir.glob('*/')
			envNames = [str(subdir.name) for subdir in subdirs]

			globalList = self.getGlobalEnvs(localEnvDir=condaInstallPath if self.Helper.validatePath(condaInstallPath) else pathlib.Path(projectFolder)) if includeGlobal else []
			
			self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload = {}
			self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload['envsName'] = envNames
			self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload['globalList'] = globalList

		self.SafeLogger.debug('Refreshed Conda Env List')

	def RefreshCondaEnvListThreaded(self) -> None:
		"""
		Refresh the list of conda environments in a thread.
		"""
		myThread = self.ThreadManager.TDTask(

			ExceptHook=self.refreshCondaEnvironmentsListExcept,
			RefreshHook=self.refreshCondaEnvironmentsListRefresh,
			args=(self.Helper.getCondaRoot(), project.folder, self.ownerComp.par.Includeglobal.eval(),)

		self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Refreshing Conda Env List...'
		self.Logger.Info(f'Refreshing Conda Env List. This could take a while.')
		return
	
	def refreshCondaEnvironmentsListSuccess(self) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the Conda environment list refresh is successful.

		If the payload is not None, it means that the conda installation 
		path was valid and the list of environments was refreshed in a thread.

		We are updating the parameter Environment menu dropdown with the new list of environments.
		We are also checking if the current environment is valid and if not, 
		we are setting it to the first available environment.
		"""
		condaInstallPath = self.Helper.getCondaRoot()

		if self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload:
			envNames = self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload['envsName']
			globalList = self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload['globalList']
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuNames=envNames + [globalEnv[1] for globalEnv in globalList]
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuLabels=envNames + [globalEnv[0] for globalEnv in globalList]
			
			if self.ownerComp.par.Environment == '':
				self.ownerComp.par.Environment = self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuNames[0] if self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuNames else ''
			
			if self.ownerComp.par.Environment not in self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuNames:
				if self.Helper.validatePath(self.Helper.getCondaEnvPath(self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval())):
					pass
				
				else:
					self.Logger.Error('Environment could not be found, setting to first available environment.')
					self.ownerComp.par.Environment = self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuNames[0] if self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuNames else self.ownerComp.par.Environment

			if envNames:
				self.Logger.Info(f'Conda Environment(s) found: {envNames + [globalEnv[0] for globalEnv in globalList]}')
			elif globalList:
				self.Logger.Info(f'Global Conda Environment(s) found: {[globalEnv[0] for globalEnv in globalList]}')
			else:
				self.Logger.Error(f'No Conda Environment(s) found.')
		
		else:
			self.Logger.Error(f'Conda Install path was not found. {condaInstallPath}. Relative environments will not be in the list.')
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuNames=[]
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment.menuLabels=[]

			if self.ownerComp.par.Includeglobal.eval():
				self.Logger.Error(f'Including global environments with matching Python version {self.Helper.pythonVersion} requires a valid Conda Install Folder to be set.')			

		self.refreshCondaEnvListPayload = None
		self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Environment linked and ready.' if self.Helper.Ready else 'Conda module not available.'
		self.Logger.Info('Environment linked and ready.' if self.Helper.Ready else 'Conda module not available.')

		self.Working = False
		self.resetUICue()

		return

	def getSuprocessCodeInfo(self, returnCode: int) -> str:
		"""
		Get the subprocess return code information.

		Args:
			returnCode (int): The return code of the subprocess.

		Returns:
			str: The message corresponding to the return code, if any was matching.
		"""
		subprocessCodesInfos = {
			0: "Process exited.",
			3221225786: 'Process closed by user.'
		}
		message = subprocessCodesInfos.get(returnCode, f"Unknown return code.")

		return message

	def SubprocessTerminated(self, returnCode: int) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the subprocess is terminated.

		Args:
			returnCode (int): The return code of the subprocess.
		"""
		if returnCode not in [0, 3221225786]:
			self.Logger.Error(f'Prompt was closed with return code {returnCode}: {self.getSuprocessCodeInfo(returnCode)}')

		else:
			self.Logger.Info(f'Prompt was closed with return code {returnCode}: {self.getSuprocessCodeInfo(returnCode)}')
		
		self.Helper.runningProcess = None
		
		self.Working = False
		self.resetUICue()

	def onPulseCreateConda(self) -> None:
		"""
		Pulse callback for creating a Conda environment.

		Multiple checks are done, including miniconda download, 
		install, conda install validation and creation of environment 
		depending on various circumstances.
		"""
		if self.ownerComp.par.Active.eval():
			condaInstallPath = self.Helper.getCondaRoot()	
			
			if self.Helper.validatePath(condaInstallPath):
				isCondaLinked = self.Helper.LinkConda(condaInstallPath)
				
				if isCondaLinked:
					self.createCondaEnvThreaded()
				else:
					result = self.condaTosDialog()
					if ['Continue', 'Abort'][result] == 'Abort':
						self.Logger.Info('User aborted the Conda install.')
						self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Conda install aborted.'
						return		
					
					self.downloadCondaThreaded()
				
			else:
				result = self.condaTosDialog()
				if ['Continue', 'Abort'][result] == 'Abort':
					self.Logger.Info('User aborted the Conda install.')
					self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Conda install aborted.'
					return
				
				self.downloadCondaThreaded()
		else:
			self.Logger.Info('Component is not active. Set Active to True to create environment.')

	"""
	def createPyEnvThreaded(self, useReq: bool = False) -> None:
		"""
		Create a Python virtual environment in a thread, using the Helper class.

		Args:
			useReq (bool, optional): Use the requirements.txt file if 
			the method was called from Createfromrequirementstxt par. Defaults to False.
		"""
		if pathlib.Path(project.folder) != self.Helper.installPath:
			# The project folder changed, likely following a save, set new installPath
			self.Helper.setPythonRoot(project.folder)
			
		if not self.Helper.verifyPython(self.Helper.installPath):
			myThread = self.ThreadManager.TDTask(
				target=self.Helper.createPythonEnv,
				SuccessHook=self.createPyEnvSuccess,
				ExceptHook=self.createPyEnvExcept,
				RefreshHook=self.createPyEnvRefresh,
				args=(self.Helper.installPath, self.Helper.envName, useReq)
			)
			self.ThreadManager.EnqueueTask(myThread)
			self.Working = True
			self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Creating Python vEnv...'
			self.Logger.Info('Creating Python vEnv...')

		return

	def createPyEnvSuccess(self) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the Python virtual environment creation is successful.
		Add the Python virtual environment to the search path.
		"""
		self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Python vEnv created successfully. Linking Python vEnv...'
		self.Logger.Info('Python vEnv created successfully. Linking Python vEnv...')
		
		projectFolderPath = pathlib.Path(project.folder)
		pyvenvFolder = projectFolderPath / (projectFolderPath.name + '_vEnv')		
		succces = self.Helper.linkEnv(pyvenvFolder)
		if succces:
			self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Python vEnv linked.'
			self.Logger.Info('Python vEnv linked.')
		else:
			self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Python vEnv could not be linked.'
			self.Logger.Error('Python vEnv could not be linked.')

		self.Helper.runningProcess = None
		
		self.Working = False
		self.resetUICue()

		self.createEnvGitIgnoreThreaded()

	def Restart(self) -> None:
		"""
		Prompt the user to save following a restart trigger, before quitting the current TouchDesigner session.
		"""
		result = ui.messageBox('Warning', 'The program is about to quit, you might lose some changes', buttons=['Save and Reload', 'Reload', 'Cancel'])
		self.Logger.Debug(result)
		if ['Save and Reload', 'Reload', 'Cancel'][result] == 'Save and Reload':
			project.save()
			project.load(project.folder + '/' + project.name)

		elif ['Save and Reload', 'Reload', 'Cancel'][result] == 'Reload':
			project.load(project.folder + '/' + project.name)
		
		else:
			return

	def rotateStatusChar(self, originalStatus: str) -> str:
		"""
		Rotate the status character in the status string based on the elapsed time.
		This is used to indicate that the process is still running.
		"""
		rotation_chars = ['/', '-', '\\', '|']
		status = originalStatus

		# Calculate the elapsed time in seconds
		elapsedTime = absTime.frame / project.cookRate
		
		# Update the status every 1 second
		if int(elapsedTime * 2) % 1 == 0:	
			# Remove any existing rotation character
			if any(char in status for char in rotation_chars):
				status = status[:-2]
			
			# Add rotation character
			rotation_index = int(elapsedTime * 2) % len(rotation_chars)
			status += ' ' + rotation_chars[rotation_index]
		
		return status

	def OnValueChangeActive(self, par, prev) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the Active parameter changes.
		
		Prompt the user with a disclaimer dialog if the parameter is set to True.
		Proceed to onInitTD() to reinitialize the component.
		
		Unlink any existing environment if the parameter is set to False.

		Args:
			par (par): The parameter that changed.
			prev (bool): The previous value of the parameter.
		"""
		if prev == False and par.eval() == True:
			disclaimerDialog = self.disclaimerDialog() if not self.Helper.loadedFromContext else 0
			if ['Continue', 'Abort'][disclaimerDialog] == 'Abort':
				par.val = False
				return
			else:
				self.postInit()

		else:
			self.Helper.unlinkEnv()
			self.Logger.Info('Component is now inactive.')
			self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Toggle the Active par to start.' 

		return
	
	def OnValueChangeMode(self, par, prev) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the Mode parameter changes.
		This will update the environment type and link the new environment if any.

		If the mode switched to Conda Env, it will also check if the conda install path is valid,
		and add the conda install path to the sys.path and os.environ['PATH'] for conda lib access.

		Args:
			par (par): The parameter that changed.
			prev (str): The previous value of the parameter.
		"""
		if prev == 'Conda Env':
			self.Helper.UnlinkConda()
		
		self.Helper.unlinkEnv(mode=prev)

		mode = par.eval()
		self.Helper.mode = mode

		projectFolderAsPath = pathlib.Path(project.folder)
		self.Helper.envName = self.Helper.validateEnvName(projectFolderAsPath.name + '_vEnv' if mode == 'Python vEnv' else self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval())
		if self.Helper.envName != self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval():
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment = self.Helper.envName
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Environment name {self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval()} was not valid. Set to {self.Helper.envName}.')
		
		if self.Helper.mode == 'Python vEnv':
			self.Helper.setPythonRoot(projectFolderAsPath)
		else:
			parPath = self.ownerComp.par.Condainstallfolder.eval() if platform.system() == 'Windows' else self.ownerComp.par.Condainstallfolder.eval().replace('\\', '/')
			condaInstallPath = self.Helper.setCondaRoot(pathlib.Path(parPath))
			
			if self.Helper.validatePath(condaInstallPath):
				isCondaLinked = self.Helper.LinkConda(self.Helper.getCondaRoot())
				if isCondaLinked:
					self.RefreshCondaEnvListThreaded()
			
			else:
				self.Logger.Error('Could not find a valid conda install. Update install path or create env to continue.')
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid conda install. Update install path or create env to continue.'
				self.RefreshCondaEnvListThreaded() # Can be necessary to clear obsolete env menu
				return
		
		envPath = self.Helper.getCondaEnvPath(self.ownerComp.par.Environment.eval()) if mode == 'Conda Env' else self.Helper.getPythonEnvPath(self.Helper.envName)
		if self.Helper.validatePath(envPath):
			success = self.Helper.linkEnv(envPath)
			if success:
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Environment linked and ready.'
				self.Logger.Info('Environment linked and ready.')
			else:
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not link environment.'
				self.Logger.Error('Could not link environment.')

		else:
			if mode == 'Python vEnv':
				self.Logger.Error(f'vEnv path {envPath} was not valid or could not be resolved. Create vEnv.')
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid vEnv, create vEnv to continue.'
			
			else:
				self.Logger.Error(f'Conda env path {envPath} was not valid or could not be resolved. Create Conda env.')
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not find a valid conda env, create env to continue.'

	def OnValueChangeCondainstallfolder(self, par, prev) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the Conda install folder parameter changes.
		This will update the conda install path and link the conda installation if valid.
		Will refresh the conda env list if the conda install path is valid.

		If the conda install path is not valid, it will unlink the conda installation.

		Args:
			par (par): The parameter that changed.
			prev (str): The previous value of the parameter.
		"""
		context = self.Helper.AsContext()
		
		if context['envPath'] and not pathlib.Path(context['envPath']).exists():
			self.Helper.unlinkEnv()
			self.Logger.Info('Current env path does not exist. Unlinking.')

		self.Helper.UnlinkConda()

		condaInstallPath = self.Helper.setCondaRoot(pathlib.Path(par.eval()))
		if self.Helper.validatePath(condaInstallPath):
			isCondaLinked = self.Helper.LinkConda(self.Helper.getCondaRoot())
			if isCondaLinked:
				self.RefreshCondaEnvListThreaded()

	def OnValueChangeEnvironment(self, par, prev) -> None:
		"""
		Callback for when the Environment parameter changes.
		This will update the environment name and link the new environment if valid.

		If the environment path was set to a previous environment,
		it will get unlinked first.

		Args:
			par (par): The parameter that changed.
			prev (str): The previous value of the parameter.
		"""
		self.Helper.unlinkEnv()
		
		envName = self.Helper.validateEnvName(par.eval())
		if envName != par.eval():
			self.ownerComp.par.Environment = envName
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Environment name {par.eval()} was not valid. Set to {envName}.')

		self.Helper.envName = envName
		envPath = self.Helper.getCondaEnvPath(envName)
		if self.Helper.validatePath(envPath):
			# Conda env is valid, link it.
			success = self.Helper.linkEnv(envPath)
			if success:
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Environment linked and ready.'
				self.Logger.Info('Environment linked and ready.')
			else:
				self.ownerComp.par.Status = 'Could not link environment.'
				self.Logger.Error('Could not link environment.')
